summary: Add Prettier configuration and npm format script
problem_statement: The codebase has no enforced formatting standard. Inconsistent whitespace, quote styles, and line lengths create noisy diffs, increase merge conflicts, and make code review harder. A single Prettier config eliminates formatting debates and ensures uniform style across all contributors.
in_scope: Install Prettier as a dev dependency
Create a Prettier configuration file at the project root with project-appropriate settings
Add an npm run format script that formats the application source in-place
Add an npm run format:check script that verifies formatting without modifying files
Ensure Prettier and ESLint do not conflict (disable ESLint formatting rules or use eslint-config-prettier)
out_of_scope: Pre-commit hook wiring — handled in P1-R2-03
Formatting generated files, node_modules, or build output
requirements: Prettier is installed as a dev dependency
npm run format:check exits with code 0 on the current codebase
ESLint and Prettier do not produce conflicting rules
acceptance_criteria: Prettier reports zero formatting violations via npm run format:check
npm run format and npm run format:check scripts exist in package.json
dependencies: P1-R2-01 — ESLint must be configured first so Prettier integration (eslint-config-prettier) can be verified
risks: Initial format run may produce a large diff touching many files; should be committed as a standalone formatting-only commit
implementation_notes: Use eslint-config-prettier to disable ESLint rules that conflict with Prettier. Consider a .prettierignore for build artifacts and generated files.
